free_text: The reaction C-12(d,n0)N-13 and C-12(d,p0)C-13 at
deuteron energies from 2.7 to 3.2 MeV.
3 MeV HVEC
The energy spread of the beam was about 2 keV.
NE-213 liquid scintillator (12.5*2.5 cm).
The relative efficiency of the scintillator was
determined by measuring the H3(P,N)HE4 and H2(D,N)HE3
neutrons, relating them to the known cross sections.
Data digitizing error
The relative errors of the efficiency are estimated to
be less than 10% for neutrons in the energy range from
1.2 to 2.8 MeV. For a single yield the error is
estimated to be less than 5%.
LVL=0 in Sub 001 was added, REACTION code
in Sub 002-005 was changed. Sub 002 was split into
three subentries. Data in Sub 002, 004 were redigitized

Fig.5
The protons were detected with two silicon
surface barrier counters, with a depletion layer of
about 500 mu and 4.5 mm diameter sensitive area. They
were mounted at 7.5 cm distance from the target.
A self-supporting target of natural carbon was used.The
target thickness was determined to be about 10 mu-g/cm2
by comparing the energy loss of alpha particles in the
target with that in the equivalent amount of air.
